intent: ADVERSARIAL parser-quoting: companion fields comm=/exe=/proctitle= carry literal spaces and embedded escaped quotes (the ausearch -i interpreted form a human pastes), and the raw-audit.log companion (denials.ausearch.txt) HEX-encodes the same values without quotes (the kernel/auditd encoding for special-char values). The parser must not split on the internal spaces, must handle the escaped inner quotes, and must decode the hex form - all WITHOUT corrupting the four SELinux fields. These fields only enrich the human explanation (f4 section 1.3); they never change the rule.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). The awkward companion fields are enrichment only; the SELinux fields are clean, so this is a plain TeAllowable read.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow logrotate_t shadow_t:file read;  (audit2allow ignores comm/exe/proctitle entirely; the spaces/quotes do not affect its output)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: omits the auth_read_shadow() macro (attribute + 5-perm shadow_t padding + 6-perm etc_t:dir). expected.te emits only the single denied `read`.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A parser that tokenizes the line by splitting on whitespace would break comm="my cat" (space inside the quotes) into two tokens, mis-aligning everything after it - potentially mis-reading scontext/tcontext or dropping the denial. Embedded escaped quotes in proctitle="... --opt=\"quoted value\" ..." compound the trap. And the raw-audit.log hex form (comm=2F62... = /bin/my cat) must be decoded, not treated as a literal token. The parser must isolate the four SELinux fields robustly regardless of how messy the enrichment fields are.
